food_subgroup: Non-carbonated bottled beverages (still water, juice, isotonic, tea-based)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301099 covers bottled non-carbonated beverages as a parent category. No single exact code exists for the generic multi-type non-carbonated bottled drink category; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers non-alcoholic beverages as a broad parent. No specific FoodEx2 leaf code for generic non-carbonated bottled drinks; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 is the closest defensible parent for non-carbonated bottled drinks as a category.

main_ingredients: may include: fruit juice, tea extract, electrolytes, natural flavours, sugar (product-dependent)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard commercially bottled non-carbonated drink, typically 500 ml, as sold at Subway and similar quick-service restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, water, tap, drinking — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; and Beverages, tea, ready-to-drink, unsweetened — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; used as representative anchors for the non-carbonated bottled drink category
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Non-carbonated bottled drinks sold at Subway Singapore encompass a range of products including still water, isotonic drinks, and unsweetened bottled tea. Nutrient values are anchored to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for plain drinking water (FDC ID 174831) and ready-to-drink unsweetened tea (FDC ID 171891) as the most common product types in this category. Per-100g values reflect a conservative category-level estimate weighted toward plain still water, which is the dominant product type. Sugar-containing variants (e.g. isotonic, juice-based) would have higher carbohydrate and energy values; this record represents the baseline still-water anchor. Confidence is low due to category breadth.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of water and non-caloric beverages — University of Sydney GI Database convention; plain water and non-caloric beverages assigned GI of effectively zero
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain still water and non-caloric non-carbonated beverages contain no glycemic carbohydrates and therefore have an effective GI of zero. Per the zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) to satisfy numeric validation requirements. gi_category is low. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g per serving is 0. Sugar-containing variants in this category (e.g. isotonic drinks, fruit juice) would have higher GI and GL values and should be recorded as separate food items.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content in plain still water variant
hypertension.guidance: Plain non-carbonated bottled water contains approximately 2 mg sodium per 100 ml and poses no meaningful sodium load for hypertensive individuals; however, flavoured or isotonic variants in this category may contain added sodium and should be checked on the individual product label.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates in plain still water variant
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain non-carbonated bottled water contains no glycemic carbohydrates and does not raise postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should verify that their chosen product is unsweetened, as sugar-containing variants in this category (e.g. isotonic drinks, juice-based beverages) can contribute significant carbohydrate load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: No fat, cholesterol, or significant sodium in plain still water variant
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain non-carbonated bottled water is free of saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol, making it a cardiovascularly neutral beverage choice; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid sweetened or high-sodium variants within this broad product category.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Non-carbonated bottled drinks is a broad commercial beverage category encompassing still (non-fizzy) packaged drinks sold in sealed bottles, as commonly offered at quick-service restaurants such as Subway in Singapore. The category includes plain still mineral water, unsweetened bottled tea, isotonic sports drinks, and fruit-juice-based beverages — all sharing the characteristic of containing no carbonation. In the Singapore quick-service restaurant context, these drinks are typically sold in 500 ml PET bottles at approximately SGD 3.30. Nutritionally, the category spans a wide range: plain still water contributes essentially zero calories, protein, fat, and carbohydrates, while isotonic and juice-based variants may contribute 20–50 kcal per 100 ml and 5–12 g of sugar per 100 ml. The baseline nutrient profile in this record is anchored to plain still water as the dominant product type. From a clinical perspective, plain non-carbonated water is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease, as it contributes no glycemic load, no saturated fat, and negligible sodium. Consumers with diabetes or metabolic conditions should verify the sugar content of individual products within this category before consumption. The glycemic index of plain water is effectively zero; the value is recorded as 1 per convention for numeric validation purposes.
common_user_queries: What drinks are available at Subway Singapore?
Is bottled water available at Subway?
Non-carbonated drink options at fast food
Is bottled tea good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Non-carbonated bottled drinks is a broad category label, not a single food item. The nutrient values are anchored to plain still water from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as the most conservative and common product type. Actual nutritional content varies significantly across products within this category. Confidence is low due to category breadth and the absence of a single canonical product definition.
notes: This record represents a category-level anchor for non-carbonated bottled drinks as sold at Subway Funan and Funan Mix & Match in Singapore. The input record incorrectly classified this item under cuisine 'Sandwich' and food_group 'Vegetables'; both have been corrected to 'Beverages'. Nutrient values are anchored to plain still water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 174831) as the dominant product type. protein_g is naturally zero as plain water contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as plain water contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero for plain still water per USDA FoodData Central. sugar_g is confirmed zero for plain still water. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero for plain still water. energy_kcal is set to 1.0 (trace) reflecting the negligible mineral content of bottled water; confirmed near-zero per USDA FoodData Central. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 since carbohydrate_g per serving is 0. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are confirmed zero for plain water. All vitamin fields set to 0.0 as plain water contains no vitamins; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to 0.0 as confirmed zero or below detection threshold for plain water per USDA FoodData Central. potassium_mg set to 1.0 mg/100g (trace, confirmed per USDA FoodData Central for tap/bottled water). calcium_mg set to 7.0 mg/100g (trace mineral content typical of bottled mineral water, per USDA FoodData Central). magnesium_mg set to 2.0 mg/100g (trace, per USDA FoodData Central). water_g set to 99.9 per USDA FoodData Central for plain drinking water. Sugar-containing variants (isotonic, juice-based) within this category should be recorded as separate food items with their own nutrient profiles. Price data preserved from input: SGD 3.30. Restaurant context preserved: Subway Funan and Funan Mix & Match.

Applied zero-macro justification phrases in notes for protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g.
Set glycemic_index to 1 per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule; documented in notes and gi_source.
Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Ensured aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent.
Populated ontology with closest_parent codes from FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT with gap notes.
Documented all sentinel and zero values in notes field.
